methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Project Omega is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Stafford Sheehan that has raised $12M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 identifiable peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median comparison is not applicable. The nuclear energy peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage) to support a robust median analysis; a broader cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise. Stage-progression applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $12M raised proxy, implying a $30M valuation. This reflects typical economics of seed-to-next-stage advancement within venture-backed deep tech.

The triangulated valuation range is $26M to $34M, with a median of $30M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression implied value. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of narrow peer multiples in nuclear energy, limiting benchmarking precision; (2) undisclosed cap table and subsequent financing terms that may alter effective valuation; and (3) regulatory and technical execution risk inherent to early-stage nuclear ventures. The single-method foundation suggests valuation should be revisited once additional seed or Series A comparables emerge in the sector.
